citeinfo.origin: REQUIRED: The name of an organization or individual that developed the data set.
citeinfo.pubdate: REQUIRED: The date when the data set is published or otherwise made available for release.
citeinfo.title: MVCA Watercourses

descript.abstract: Shapefile containing information pertaining to all streams and waterways within the Maitland Valley Watershed. Includes drains and natural watercourses. DFO code outlines the stream class.
descript.purpose: Watercourses for all MVCA. Valuable for alteration to watercourse permits. Last updated May 5, 2017.

theme.themekt: REQUIRED: Reference to a formally registered thesaurus or a similar authoritative source of theme keywords.

idinfo.accconst: For internal use by MVCA staff and partners
idinfo.useconst: "The MVCA owns and retains all Intellectual Property (IP) on this data. Additional statements may include but not limited to: "Data share agreement needed when distributing data."; Disclaimer "Layer streaml8k_adjust_utrca_v2.shp provided by the Maitland Valley Conservation Authority. It represents best available information and is subject to change".
idinfo.native: Microsoft Windows Vista Version 6.1 (Build 7601) Service Pack 1; ESRI ArcCatalog 9.3.1.4000

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

distinfo.distliab: "The data is provided "as is" without any guarantee, representation, condition or warranty of any kind, either expressed, implied or statutory, including but not limited to the implied warranties and representations of merchantability and fitness for a particular purpose. The MVCA, its directors, employees and agents are not liable to the user of the data for any direct, indirect, special, consequential or exemplary damages, or damages of any nature."

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standards for Digital Geospatial Metadata

lineage.Process: CalculateField "Base Data\Water\Watercourse" NAME "Nine Mile River" VB #

dataIdInfo.idPurp: Watercourses for all MVCA. Valuable for alteration to watercourse permits. Last updated March 14, 2019.
dataIdInfo.idAbs:
Shapefile containing information pertaining to all streams and waterways within the Maitland Valley Watershed. Includes drains and natural watercourses.

SOURCES INCLUDE: LIO_CONSTRUCTED_DRAINS (MNRF - 2018); OHN (2018); DFO_SARSSegments_2014.shp; Historic MVCA stream layer - streamcl8k_adjust_utrca.shp

DFO code outlines the stream class(2017), please see: https://www.dsao.net/images/Documents/Dart/General/Guidance-for-Maintaining-and-Repairing-Municipal-Drains-in-Ontario-March-7-2017-V1.0.pdf

DFO_CODE:

A – Permanent, Fall activity, No sensitive fish species present

B – Permanent, Spring activity, Sensitive fish species present, Cleaned out within 10 years

C – Permanent, Spring activity, No sensitive fish species present

D – Permanent, Fall activity, Sensitive fish species present

E – Intermittent

NR - Unrated

Draintype_2018:

Closed - a Closed Municipal Drain. (Source: Drainage Superintendant, engineered drawing, SWOOP 2015)

Open - a Open Municipal Drain. (Source: Drainage Superintendant, engineered drawing, SWOOP 2015)

Private - a privately-owned closed/tiled in drain. (Source: historic data, SWOOP 2015)

Natural - a natural occuring watercourse OR an open privately-owned drain. (Source: historic data, SWOOP 2015)

Abandoned - an old drain/watercourse that has been "moved" or filled in. (Source: historic data, SWOOP 2015)

Unknown - outside of MVCA Watershed boundaries or the portion of the drain/watercourse that ends in Lake Huron

Consts.useLimit: "The MVCA owns and retains all Intellectual Property (IP) on this data. Additional statements may include but not limited to: "Data share agreement needed when distributing data."; Disclaimer "Layer MVCA_Watercourses_CURRENT provided by the Maitland Valley Conservation Authority. It represents best available information and is subject to change".
